hard.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - fu on RFI and timelines 6.15.26 KK - rfi submitted 6.11.26 KK -received RFI with submission 6.15. once downselected will move to formal RFP
hard.ais_why:
• Nubank's business objectives explicitly include 'Enable AI-powered analytics for supplier selection, risk scoring, and spend optimization,' a clear AI-oriented goal.
• ANA and Merlin are scoped and the deal is at Formal Evaluation via an RFI process, so AI analytics is a stated evaluation objective.
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and the broader objectives emphasize efficiency, compliance and scalability, so AI is one of several drivers rather than the dominant one.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus cleared Nubank's RFI evaluation (2 Jul) and advances toward a formal RFP, but only the supplier slice is in play and an RFP-to-contract runway cannot close by 21 Aug
north_star_verdict.math: Close 21 Aug 2026 (46 days out). Created 21 Aug 2025, ~11 months old. In Formal Evaluation since 11 Jun 2026 (~25 days). RFI received 11 Jun, submitted 15 Jun, intro call with new EB Eduardo Freitas 18 Jun, rep RFI follow-up 29 Jun, and inbound 2 Jul 2026 email confirming Zycus passed the RFI evaluation. Remaining path (formal RFP, downselect, deeper demos, pricing, EB/CFO sign-off, legal) is a 3-5 month enterprise motion, so the 21 Aug date will slip while the deal itself is healthy and advancing.
evidence: Inbound 2 Jul 2026 email: 'following the conclusion of our RFI evaluation, we are pleased to inform you that Zycus has been' selected to advance (Clari email received, Task 2 Jul 2026)

Eduardo Freitas (Procurement Director, new EB) intro call 18 Jun 2026: 'this RFI ongoing is the only one we already defined to go to the market... compiling the answers to get a short list and the ones we are going to continue'
RFI submitted 15 Jun 2026 with rep follow-ups on timelines 29 Jun; buyer inbound 12 Jun and 2 Jul confirm live two-way engagement
Eduardo confirmed buy-from-market intent for the sourcing piece: 'we are not going to build in house eSourcing, eRequest... we should get one solution from the market' (18 Jun 2026)
No pricing proposal issued, no EB/CFO joint commercial call, RFP not yet released, and no mutual close plan against the 21 Aug date

metrics.narrative: The core business problem is confirmed and vivid: Eduardo Freitas described the disconnect between negotiated contract value and payments as 'a black box, total black box' and said 'the weakness is high' because there is 'no connection' between contracting and payment (18 Jun 2026). He also framed procurement as 'a very low maturity area' doing tactical ticket regularization rather than strategic sourcing. What is missing is hard quantification: no savings target, cycle-time, or ROI figure has been put on the pain, so the value case rests on qualitative agility and visibility arguments. Quantifying supplier-onboarding cycle time and Maverick/tail spend under management should be the next value step.
sources: Eduardo Freitas (Procurement Director), 18 Jun 2026 intro call

economic_buyer.narrative: Eduardo Freitas, Procurement Director (OpportunityContactRole), is the economic buyer: he joined ~3 months before the 18 Jun 2026 call as Nubank's first dedicated procurement director and stated 'we as new bank decide to have a proper director for procurement, so a dedicated one', controlling the vendor selection and the RFI-to-RFP process. He reports to the CFO, who forwarded him the Zycus Horizon event invitation ('the first invitation that I got, I got from my boss, the CFO of New Bank'), so the CFO is an aware but unmapped executive sponsor. Eduardo is engaged and commercially fluent (he probed the SaaS pricing model, per-module metrics, and build-vs-buy directly), but no pricing proposal or CFO joint call has happened, so his budget ceiling and any required CFO co-sign are still unconfirmed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging from the RFI and the demos: the RFI itself targets supplier qualification and onboarding (the 'SRM RFI', top-left iSupplier/iRisk piece per Eduardo, 18 Jun), and stated evaluation drivers are user-experience/adoption (Ariba is 'super allergic' to Nubank's agile culture), end-to-end integration into their Jira/Slack/Netlex/SAP stack, AI-driven automation (Merlin is 'one of the key focus of new bank's evaluation', Ronit 26 Sep), and price-versus-features fit ('understanding the composition of price versus features... as simple as possible', Eduardo 18 Jun). Nathalia Pais added supplier performance/SLA management as a criterion (26 Sep). These are confirmed as themes but not yet a formal weighted scorecard, which the formal RFP should crystallize.
sources: Nathalia Pais and Ronit Papa, 26 Sep 2025 demo

decision_process.narrative: The process is buyer-run and legible from the call sequence: Nubank launched a structured SRM RFI (invite 3 Jun, clarifications 8-12 Jun, responses due/submitted 15 Jun), is now compiling responses into a shortlist, and per Eduardo 'once downselected will move to formal RFP' (Next_Step, 29 Jun). Zycus passed the RFI evaluation on 2 Jul, so an RFP round and deeper use-case demos come next. Procurement coordinators Brenda Ayres and Felicia manage RFI correspondence, Eduardo owns the selection, and the CFO sits above him. What is not yet visible is the approval chain and signature authority for a purchase, which needs to be mapped before pricing.
sources: RFI email thread (Brenda Ayres / Felicia, 3-12 Jun 2026)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are genuinely unknown at this early stage: no MSA, Order Form, SOW, or InfoSec track has begun, and there is no current-contract-expiration forcing function on record (Nubank runs Ariba today but no renewal date is captured). For a BFSI buyer in Brazil, expect InfoSec/security review, DPA/GDPR-equivalent (LGPD) data-processing terms, and SAP/Google Workspace integration scoping to become gating once a vendor is selected. Visibility here will only come after the RFP downselect; the move is to ask Eduardo for the intended procurement, legal, and security review path as part of RFP engagement.
sources: Absence across SF fields, activities, and calls as of 6 Jul 2026

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and owned by Eduardo Freitas: Ariba is a poor cultural fit ('we receive a lot of complaints, Ariba is a difficult tool to customize... the organization will not be adapted to a tool', 18 Jun), sourcing is run manually over email ('nowadays we do everything through email, we don't use any iSource'), and the contract-to-payment gap is unmanaged ('the lack of connections... is a black box'). Nathalia Pais and Giulliana Canesin confirmed operational contract-review pain (clause-specific reviews, manual metadata, HR/delegation-of-authority workflows) in the 26 Sep 2025 demo. The pain owner is procurement (Eduardo) with legal-ops (Giulliana) and legal (Nathalia) as affected stakeholders.
sources: Nathalia Pais / Giulliana Canesin, 26 Sep 2025 demo

champion.narrative: There is no confirmed active champion. Ligia Alvarenga, the former head of procurement, drove the Aug-Sep 2025 discovery and demos but has not appeared on a call since 26 Sep 2025 and her current role is unknown after Eduardo's hire reset the reporting line. Eduardo Freitas is receptive and transparent but he is the economic buyer evaluating several options (Zycus, Coupa, build-in-house), not yet an advocate pushing internally for Zycus. The deal is effectively single-threaded through Eduardo with legal-side influencers (Giulliana, Nathalia) who have not re-engaged in 2026. Building a genuine internal champion who will carry Zycus through the RFP is the highest-leverage relationship move.
sources: Ligia Alvarenga last on 26 Sep 2025 demo

competition.narrative: This is a multi-front evaluation. SAP Ariba is the incumbent being displaced for poor UX and rigidity (Eduardo: 'we are using Ariba... a lot of complaints... difficult tool to customize', 18 Jun), so it is a low current threat. Coupa is named as a market peer Eduardo considered ('I need to implement here maybe a Coupa or an Ariba') and Karson positioned Zycus against 'Coupa, Jaggaer, Ariba' in the Gartner top-right, making Coupa the live commercial peer. The strongest strategic threat is build-in-house: Eduardo, shaped by Nubank's Silicon Valley engineering culture, is 'thinking more about build internally our process or solutions' for some modules (spend analytics, contract), while explicitly conceding they will buy iSourcing/eRequest from the market. The wedge is speed-to-value and TCO of an organic single-codebase S2P with Merlin orchestration versus a slow internal build and a clunky Ariba.
sources: Eduardo Freitas and Karson Keogh, 18 Jun 2026 call

deal_movement.summary: Deal is advancing on the buyer's RFI clock: new EB Eduardo Freitas re-engaged 18 Jun after an 8-month gap, Zycus submitted the RFI 15 Jun, and the buyer confirmed on 2 Jul that Zycus cleared the RFI evaluation and will advance toward a formal RFP. Trajectory is stronger versus the prior sweep.

items.change: Zycus passed Nubank's RFI evaluation and is advancing (inbound email)
Rep follow-up on RFI status and downselect timelines
Intro/qualification call with new economic buyer Eduardo Freitas
RFI response submitted to Nubank procurement
Stage moved Initial Interest to Formal Evaluation

competitive_position.summary: Build-in-house is the single strongest CURRENT threat: Eduardo Freitas, shaped by Nubank's engineering-led culture, is 'thinking more about build internally' for several modules (18 Jun 2026), though he conceded iSourcing/eRequest will be bought from the market. Coupa is the live commercial peer named by Eduardo and positioned by Zycus in the Gartner top-right (18 Jun). SAP Ariba is the incumbent actively being displaced for poor UX and rigidity, so it is the most-mentioned but weakest current threat. Jaggaer was named only as a market label with no Nubank lean.

competitors.name: Build in-house
competitors.quote: this whole mindset in this Silicon Valley of the software industries in disruption, it's much easier to create your own software... myself as a learner is thinking more about build internally our process or solutions
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify speed-to-value and TCO: Zycus SaaS deploy in 3-6 months plus continuous R&D and pre-built SAP/Jira/Slack connectors versus 12-18 months of internal build that distracts from Nubank's core fintech product; position AppExtend co-innovation as the middle path so they get custom flexibility without build risk.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: your main competitors like Coupa, Jaggaer, Ariba, this is the marketing you are playing right?
competitors.source: Eduardo Freitas and Karson Keogh, 18 Jun 2026 call
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on single organic codebase versus Coupa's acquisition-built stack, Merlin as core AI (intake orchestration plus autonomous negotiation agent) versus Coupa AI bolt-ons, and provide a side-by-side TCO model plus LATAM references.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: we are using Ariba for this and quite honestly we receive a lot of complaints. Ariba is a difficult tool to customize... the organization will not be adapted to a tool, it should be the opposite side
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus's single-codebase, low-bureaucracy UX and Merlin intake (Teams/Slack/link) with Ariba's clunky, hard-to-customize experience; prove agile configuration and adoption for Nubank's fast-moving culture.

competitors.name: Jaggaer
competitors.quote: your main competitors like Coupa, Jaggaer, Ariba, this is the marketing you are playing
competitors.source: Karson Keogh / Eduardo Freitas, 18 Jun 2026 call
competitors.how_we_win: Named only as a market label with no Nubank interest; monitor and do not over-invest unless it surfaces in the RFP shortlist.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Nubank wants an agile, low-bureaucracy, AI-driven, deeply integrated supplier and contract platform. Zycus is well aligned on UX, Merlin AI, and single-codebase integration, but exposed on build-in-house preference, Google Workspace (not Microsoft Teams) as their collaboration layer, and a not-yet-quantified commercial fit.

items.quote: the culture of the new bank is super agile and entrepreneur... avoid bureaucracy... when we have to implement a S2P old school, difficult, it's super allergic

items.criterion: AI-driven automation across sourcing, contracts and risk
items.quote: Merlin is also one of the key focus of a new bank's evaluation

items.criterion: Native collaboration on Google Workspace, not Microsoft Teams
items.quote: Google. We use Google [Karson: I know Google's harder because it's the desktop version versus the app]
items.source: Eduardo Freitas and Karson Keogh, 18 Jun 2026 call

items.criterion: Buy-from-market versus build-in-house for parts of the suite
items.quote: we are not going to build in house eSourcing, eRequest... we should get one solution from the market, but there are modules we are going to probably build in house

items.requirement: Show supplier performance/SLA management in a follow-up use-case demo

items.grounding_quote: I'll send this to you, and then I'll send you just the more details on the event so you can float it to your colleague

items.deliverable: Issue the formal RFP and confirm the downselect shortlist and timeline
items.grounding_quote: once downselected will move to formal RFP

the first invitation I got, I got from my boss, the CFO of New Bank

best_practice_check.summary: Zycus just cleared the RFI; the next 30 days are about converting a receptive economic buyer into an advocate, neutralizing build-in-house, and getting a defensible commercial in front of the RFP.
flags: Neutralize build-in-house now: bring a TCO and speed-to-value model plus AppExtend co-innovation to Eduardo before the RFP locks scope
Multi-thread beyond Eduardo: re-engage Ligia and the legal-ops pair (Giulliana, Nathalia) so the deal is not single-threaded through one 3-month-tenure director
Map the CFO and the approval/signature chain early, since the CFO already sponsored awareness via the Horizon invite and likely co-signs a $100k purchase

items.name: Eduardo Freitas
items.title: Procurement Director
items.risk: Single-threaded, ~3 months tenure; if he stalls or exits, the deal has no internal advocate, and his budget ceiling and any CFO co-sign requirement are unconfirmed
items.sentiment: Receptive and transparent, but evaluating Zycus against Coupa and build-in-house; commercially fluent, no pricing commitment yet

items.name: Giulliana Canesin
items.title: Legal Operations Specialist
items.risk: No economic-buyer access or deal-driving power; not re-engaged in 2026
items.sentiment: Engaged influencer on contract review, integrations and approval-workflow/HR-system questions in the 26 Sep 2025 demo

items.name: Nathalia Pais
items.title: Legal Manager
items.risk: No economic-buyer access or budget authority; not re-engaged in 2026
items.sentiment: Engaged influencer; raised supplier performance/SLA, AI clause analysis and process-visibility requirements in the 26 Sep 2025 demo

champion_strength.summary: No active champion. Ligia Alvarenga was the Aug-Sep 2025 advocate but has been silent for 9+ months, and Eduardo Freitas is a receptive economic buyer weighing multiple options rather than an internal advocate. Building a champion who will carry Zycus through the RFP is the priority; what is developing is Eduardo's engagement, what is missing is anyone pushing Zycus internally against build-in-house.
champion_strength.champion: Ligia Alvarenga (former Head of Procurement, current role unknown)
champion_strength.source: Ligia last on 26 Sep 2025 demo; Eduardo Freitas, 18 Jun 2026 call

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin was foregrounded as 'one of the key focus of new bank's evaluation' and Zycus demoed intake orchestration, autonomous negotiation, and AI contract metadata/risk extraction. Positioning is strong; the risk is that Nubank's engineers believe they can build comparable AI themselves, so the agentic depth and R&D moat must be made concrete.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Moderate is corroborated by the calls: Nubank started an org-wide AI transformation in March 2026 and Merlin is a named evaluation focus, but AI sits alongside efficiency, integration and compliance rather than dominating, and the buyer's AI enthusiasm partly fuels a build-in-house temptation.

items.detail: Build-in-house preference for several modules could shrink or defer the purchase
CFO co-sign likely for $100k but the CFO is unmapped and Zycus has no direct relationship
RFP, downselect, pricing and legal cannot realistically close by the 21 Aug close date
Nubank runs Google Workspace not Microsoft Teams, where Merlin intake is strongest; Slack/link support must be proven
items.source: Eduardo Freitas / Karson Keogh, 18 Jun 2026 call

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and warming: two-way email traffic through 2 Jul, a new engaged economic buyer, and a positive RFI-evaluation outcome. The watch item is single-threading and an aggressive close date.
items: Inbound RFI-pass email 2 Jul 2026 (buyer-initiated positive signal)
New EB Eduardo Freitas engaged and transparent on 18 Jun 2026
Single-threaded through Eduardo with no active champion (watch)

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus just cleared Nubank's RFI evaluation and heads into a formal RFP with an engaged new economic buyer, but only the supplier slice is committed to market, build-in-house is the live rival for the rest, and there is no internal champion carrying us.

win_position.text: Zycus is advancing after Nubank confirmed on 2 Jul 2026 that we passed the RFI evaluation, with the economic buyer Eduardo Freitas engaged and Ariba being actively displaced for poor fit.
The strongest rival is Nubank building in-house: Eduardo is 'thinking more about build internally our process or solutions' (18 Jun), which could shrink Zycus to just the supplier module.
The deal is single-threaded through a 3-month-tenure director with no active champion since Ligia Alvarenga went quiet after 26 Sep 2025, and the CFO who likely co-signs is unmapped.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is rising: a new senior economic buyer joined on 18 Jun, the RFI was submitted 15 Jun, and the buyer sent a positive RFI-pass email on 2 Jul 2026.
The next milestone (formal RFP and downselect) is buyer-owned and undated, so momentum depends on Nubank releasing the RFP on their own clock.

customer_commitment.text: Nubank committed to going to market for the supplier piece: Eduardo said 'we are not going to build in house eSourcing, eRequest, we should get one solution from the market' (18 Jun).
No pricing proposal, mutual close plan, or CFO commercial conversation exists, so commitment beyond the supplier RFI is unproven.

deal_risk.text: The 21 Aug 2026 close is not credible with the RFP not yet issued and 3-5 months of enterprise motion remaining.
Scope has effectively narrowed to iSupplier/iRisk versus the full S2C suite, a defensive signal that Nubank may phase or self-build the rest.

champion.evidence: No active champion; Ligia silent since 26 Sep 2025 and Eduardo is EB not advocate

timeline.evidence: RFP not issued; 21 Aug close cannot hold

customer_preference.evidence: Passed RFI 2 Jul and Ariba being displaced, but build-in-house and Coupa still in play

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Contract suite (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iRisk, Merlin, ANA)
scope_change.to: Supplier qualification and onboarding (iSupplier + iRisk) via the active SRM RFI, with sourcing bought separately and other modules candidate for in-house build
scope_change.detail: Eduardo scoped the live evaluation to the supplier piece: 'this RFI ongoing is the only one we already defined to go to the market' and 'there are modules that we are going to probably build in house' (18 Jun 2026), narrowing near-term scope from the full S2C suite on the opportunity.

critical_signals.text: Nubank confirmed on 2 Jul 2026 that Zycus passed its RFI evaluation and will advance toward a formal RFP, the strongest buy signal on the deal so far.
The real rival is Nubank's own engineering culture: Eduardo is leaning toward building parts of the platform in-house, while Ariba is being displaced and Coupa is a market peer.
Eduardo Freitas, Nubank's first dedicated Procurement Director and the economic buyer, is newly engaged and reports directly to the CFO who sponsored the initial contact.
Nubank runs on Google Workspace rather than Microsoft Teams, so Merlin intake's collaboration integration via Slack or a web link must be proven for adoption.
No pricing has been put forward yet against a $100k opportunity, and Eduardo has explicitly asked for the price-versus-features composition for the supplier scope.

items.action: Confirm the RFP scope and timeline with Eduardo and pre-position Zycus's supplier fit.
items.trigger: Nubank confirmed Zycus passed the RFI evaluation and will move to a formal RFP
items.expected_effect: Locks Zycus into the RFP round on strong footing and surfaces the real decision timeline versus the 21 Aug date

items.action: Deliver a TCO and speed-to-value case for buy-versus-build to Eduardo before the RFP.
items.trigger: Eduardo is 'thinking more about build internally our process or solutions'
items.expected_effect: Neutralizes the build-in-house threat and protects full-suite scope against a phased self-build

items.action: Re-engage Ligia and the legal-ops pair to build a multi-threaded champion base.
items.trigger: Deal single-threaded through Eduardo; Ligia and legal influencers silent since 26 Sep 2025
items.expected_effect: Removes single-point-of-failure risk and creates an internal advocate for the RFP

items.action: Run a tailored use-case demo covering supplier onboarding and SLA performance for Nathalia.
items.trigger: Nathalia requested supplier performance/SLA management coverage
items.expected_effect: Proves fit on the exact supplier criteria in the RFP and deepens legal-side sponsorship

items.action: Prepare the price-versus-features commercial for the supplier scope and map the CFO sign-off path.
items.trigger: Eduardo asked for price-versus-features composition; CFO likely co-signs a $100k purchase
items.expected_effect: Gives Eduardo a defensible commercial for the RFP and pre-clears the executive approval chain

customer_preference.evidence: Passed the RFI (2 Jul) and asked Zycus-specific pricing/model and agent questions on 18 Jun, but no decisive lean and still weighing Coupa and build-in-house

business_case.evidence: Strong qualitative pain (Ariba unfit, contract-to-payment 'black box', manual email sourcing) but no quantified savings, cycle-time, or ROI target

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Eduardo scoped the live evaluation to the supplier piece: 'this RFI ongoing is the only one we already defined to go to the market' and 'there are modules that  (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.

commentary.win_position: Win 32 is carried by momentum 64 vs stage-expected 52; exec access strength +1.00 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Eduardo scoped the live evaluation to the supplier piece: 'this RFI ongoing is the only one we already defined….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 64 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 25 reflects real customer investment: Direct economic-buyer access recorded.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 29 is anchored on the stage win (32), nudged by commitment 25 and momentum 64, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
